phases.goal: Establish baseline habits and eliminate caloric surplus

steps.title: Establish caloric baseline
steps.description: Track all food intake for 7 days using MyFitnessPal to determine current average calorie consumption. Do not change eating habits yet — just observe.
steps.success_metric: 7 consecutive days of food logging completed

steps.title: Set a moderate caloric deficit
steps.description: Target 500 kcal/day deficit (1 lb/week loss). Use TDEE calculator to determine maintenance calories. Prioritize protein at 0.8g per lb of bodyweight.
steps.success_metric: Hitting calorie and protein targets 5 out of 7 days

steps.title: Begin 3x/week movement routine
steps.description: Start with low-intensity cardio: 30-minute walks, cycling, or swimming. The goal is habit formation, not performance. Consistency beats intensity at this stage.

phases.goal: Add strength training, optimize nutrition, accelerate fat loss

steps.title: Add 2x/week strength training
steps.description: Begin a beginner program (StrongLifts 5x5 or Starting Strength). Compound movements (squat, deadlift, bench, row) build metabolic rate and preserve muscle during a deficit.
steps.success_metric: All three main lifts progressing week-over-week

steps.title: Optimize meal timing and prep
steps.description: Batch cook proteins and vegetables on Sundays. Pre-logging meals the night before prevents impulsive choices. Eat protein-first at every meal.
steps.success_metric: Meal prep done 3 out of 4 Sundays per month

steps.title: Track weekly weigh-ins
steps.description: Weigh daily, log the 7-day average. Ignore day-to-day fluctuations (water retention, glycogen). A 0.5–1 lb/week average loss is the target range.

phases.goal: Reach target weight range and build sustainable lifestyle

steps.title: Transition to maintenance calories
steps.description: Increase calories by 200–300 kcal/day over 2 weeks. Continue training. Monitor weight weekly — target stable within 2 lb window.

steps.title: Identify and reinforce sustainable habits
steps.description: List the 3 habits that drove the most results. Build these into a permanent routine. Celebrate the process, not just the outcome number on the scale.
